refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall automatically derive performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from worksite mission context and operational parameters, record them in a traceable baseline, and maintain a managed history of all requirement updates and calibration changes with full audit trail.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was vague on 'method' and lacked explicit traceability and history management. Refinement adds specificity: (1) automatic derivation from mission context, (2) explicit baseline recording, (3) managed history with audit trail. This aligns with acceptance criteria demanding documented performance targets traceable to worksite inputs and maintained baselines/calibration updates. High safety criticality demands explicit safety margin derivation and audit capability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall confirm: (a) performance requirements are automatically computed from worksite mission inputs (e.g., terrain, payload, cycle count); (b) baseline is recorded with timestamp, source, and derivation method; (c) history log captures all updates with reason, timestamp, and approver; (d) traceability chain from worksite input → derived requirement → baseline → history is complete and auditable; (e) safety margins are explicitly calculated and documented.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Ingest worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints) from Site Manager input.
CR-NGHE-005.2: Compute performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) using mission-context-driven algorithms.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Record derived performance requirements as a baseline with metadata (timestamp, source, derivation method, approver).
CR-NGHE-005.4: Maintain a managed history of all performance requirement baselines and calibration updates with full audit trail (who, what, when, why).
CR-NGHE-005.5: Provide traceability reporting linking worksite inputs → derived requirements → baselines → history.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized Performance Derivation Engine – A dedicated software module that ingests worksite mission parameters, applies deterministic algorithms (physics-based cycle time estimation, energy models, availability calculations), and outputs a structured performance requirement set. Baseline and history managed in a versioned configuration database with audit logging.

DP-B: Distributed Derivation with Local Caching – Performance derivation logic embedded in Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, each computing requirements locally based on cached mission context. Baselines synchronized to a central registry with eventual consistency.

DP-C: Manual Derivation with Template Library – Site Manager manually selects performance templates from a library, adjusts parameters via UI, and submits for approval. History tracked via document management system.

DP-D: Hybrid Autonomous + Manual Override – Centralized engine derives requirements automatically; Site Manager and Safety Manager can override or adjust via approval workflow. All changes logged with rationale.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Hybrid Autonomous + Manual Override – Centralized Performance Derivation Engine with human-in-the-loop override capability. The engine automatically derives requirements from worksite mission context using physics-based and empirical models. Site Manager and Safety Manager can review, adjust, and approve changes. All derivations, overrides, and approvals are logged with full audit trail in a versioned baseline repository.

potential_sources: DP-D couples performance derivation (autonomous) with approval workflow (manual). This is intentional and justified by high safety criticality: autonomous derivation ensures consistency and speed; manual override ensures safety and operational context are honored.

DP-D couples the derivation engine to mission context ingestion (CR-NGHE-005.1) and history management (CR-NGHE-005.4). These are necessary couplings; the engine must read mission inputs and write to the history log.
DP-D does not couple to Vehicle or Teleoperation operational logic; it is a planning/configuration service, not a real-time control loop. Low coupling risk.
coupling_check.notes: Coupling is minimal and justified. The hybrid approach (autonomous + manual) is appropriate for high-safety-criticality systems. No hidden or problematic couplings detected.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Worksite mission context is available in structured form (e.g., terrain map, payload mass, cycle count target). If inputs are unstructured or incomplete, derivation accuracy degrades.

ASSUMPTION: Physics-based and empirical models for cycle time, energy, and availability are validated and maintained. Model drift or obsolescence will cause derived requirements to diverge from reality.
RISK: If approval workflow is slow or bureaucratic, operational agility is reduced. Mitigation: streamline approval for routine updates; escalate only significant changes.
RISK: If audit trail becomes too verbose, it may be ignored or not reviewed. Mitigation: summarize changes; highlight deviations from previous baseline.
RISK: Safety Manager and Site Manager may have conflicting views on acceptable performance margins. Mitigation: establish clear decision rules and escalation path in approval workflow.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-D is sound. Recommend clarifying the approval workflow (who approves what, under what conditions) and defining the performance derivation algorithms (cycle time model, energy model, availability model) as part of detailed design. Consider adding a 'what-if' simulation capability to allow Site Manager to explore performance trade-offs before committing to a baseline.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system integration boundary. It owns the Performance Derivation Engine (DP-D), the baseline repository, and the audit log. It ingests worksite mission context via Tasking interface, coordinates with Safety Services for safety margin validation, and publishes derived performance requirements via Telemetry interface. It also maintains the managed history of baselines and calibration updates.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints) from Site Manager or planning system. Used to drive performance requirement derivation.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends derived safety margins and performance constraints to Safety Services for validation. Receives safety-critical feedback (e.g., margin violations, constraint conflicts) that may trigger re-derivation or override.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Publishes derived performance requirements (cycle time target, energy efficiency target, availability target, safety margins) to Vehicle and Teleoperation for operational use. Also publishes baseline metadata and history summaries for audit and traceability.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Provides connectivity for real-time synchronization of mission context updates, approval workflow notifications, and audit log replication across distributed sites (if applicable).

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level integration boundary and the natural owner of cross-cutting concerns like performance derivation, baseline management, and audit logging. The Performance Derivation Engine, baseline repository, and audit log are all system-level services that should not be scattered across Vehicle or Teleoperation. High containment, minimal diffusion.

Vehicle is a physical/operational module focused on motion, actuation, and real-time control. Performance derivation is a planning/configuration service, not a vehicle-level concern. If embedded in Vehicle, it would be tightly coupled to vehicle-specific parameters and difficult to reuse or update. High diffusion risk.

Teleoperation is a remote operation and monitoring module. Performance derivation is a system-level planning service, not a teleoperation-specific concern. If embedded in Teleoperation, it would be duplicated or inconsistent across different teleoperation instances. High diffusion risk.

Safety Services owns safety validation and constraint enforcement. Performance derivation includes safety margin calculation, which overlaps with Safety Services. However, the full derivation engine (cycle time, energy, availability) is not a safety-specific concern. Partial containment; recommend NGHE Solution owns derivation engine, Safety Services validates safety margins via SafetyAlert interface.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Performance Derivation Engine, baseline repository, and audit log are system-level services that belong in the NGHE Solution module. They are not vehicle-specific, not teleoperation-specific, and not purely safety-specific. They are cross-cutting concerns that must be centralized to ensure consistency, traceability, and auditability. The NGHE Solution module will own these services and expose them via well-defined interfaces (Tasking, SafetyAlert, Telemetry, Network Link) to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services.
containment_risks: RISK: If NGHE Solution becomes a monolithic 'god module' with too many responsibilities, it may become difficult to test, deploy, and evolve. MITIGATION: Decompose NGHE Solution into logical sub-modules (Performance Derivation Engine, Baseline Repository, Audit Log, Approval Workflow) in the next decomposition level. Define clear interfaces between sub-modules.

RISK: If the Performance Derivation Engine is tightly coupled to specific mission models or vehicle types, it may not generalize to new scenarios. MITIGATION: Design the engine with pluggable model libraries and parameterizable algorithms. Validate models against real-world data.

RISK: If the audit log grows unbounded, it may consume excessive storage and slow down queries. MITIGATION: Implement log rotation, archival, and indexing strategies. Define retention policies aligned with regulatory and operational requirements.

RISK: If the approval workflow is not well-integrated with Safety Services, safety-critical changes may bypass safety review. MITIGATION: Enforce mandatory SafetyAlert interface for all performance requirement changes. Define escalation rules for safety-critical overrides.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: IM assignment is sound. NGHE Solution is the correct owner. Recommend: (1) In next decomposition level, break down NGHE Solution into Performance Derivation Engine, Baseline Repository, Audit Log, and Approval Workflow sub-modules with clear interfaces. (2) Define the SafetyAlert interface protocol in detail, including safety margin validation rules and override escalation. (3) Specify the Tasking interface schema for mission context (terrain, payload, cycle targets, constraints). (4) Design the Telemetry interface to include baseline metadata and history summaries for traceability.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-D (Hybrid Autonomous + Manual Override) directly addresses all sub-FRs: (1) Autonomous derivation engine ingests mission context (CR-NGHE-005.1), (2) engine computes performance requirements using models (CR-NGHE-005.2), (3) baseline recording with metadata is explicit (CR-NGHE-005.3), (4) managed history with audit trail is a core feature (CR-NGHE-005.4), (5) traceability reporting is enabled by the structured baseline and history (CR-NGHE-005.5). The hybrid approach (autonomous + manual override) is justified by high safety criticality. No gaps or misalignments detected.

diffusion_risks: LOW RISK: DP-D is assigned to NGHE Solution, which is the system-level integration boundary. No diffusion across Vehicle or Teleoperation. The Performance Derivation Engine, baseline repository, and audit log are centralized and owned by a single module.
LOW RISK: External interfaces (Tasking, SafetyAlert, Telemetry, Network Link) are well-defined and use known interface names. No ad-hoc or undocumented interfaces.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution has a containment score of 95 for this DP. The module boundary is clear: NGHE Solution owns the derivation engine and baseline management; Vehicle and Teleoperation consume derived requirements via Telemetry. Safety Services validates safety margins via SafetyAlert. This is a clean separation of concerns with minimal coupling.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: HIGH: The refined FR is specific and testable. Verification can confirm: (a) performance requirements are automatically derived from mission context (test with sample mission inputs), (b) baseline is recorded with metadata (inspect baseline repository), (c) history log captures all updates (inspect audit log), (d) traceability chain is complete (trace from worksite input to derived requirement to baseline to history), (e) safety margins are calculated (inspect derivation engine logic). All verification activities are feasible within NGHE Solution module scope.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: HIGH: The FR is clearly allocated to NGHE Solution. Sub-FRs are allocated to specific functions within the module (derivation engine, baseline repository, audit log, approval workflow). External dependencies (mission context from Tasking, safety validation via SafetyAlert) are explicit. No ambiguity or overlap with other modules.

recommended_revisions: REVISION 1: Clarify the approval workflow in DP-D. Define who approves what (e.g., Site Manager approves routine updates, Safety Manager approves safety-critical changes), under what conditions, and what happens if approval is denied. This will inform the detailed design of the Approval Workflow sub-module.

REVISION 2: Specify the performance derivation algorithms (cycle time model, energy model, availability model) at a high level. This will inform the detailed design of the Performance Derivation Engine and help validate assumptions about model accuracy and maintenance.

REVISION 3: Define the baseline repository schema and audit log format. This will inform the detailed design of the Baseline Repository and Audit Log sub-modules and ensure compatibility with traceability and compliance requirements.

REVISION 4: Specify the SafetyAlert interface protocol in detail, including safety margin validation rules, constraint conflict resolution, and override escalation. This will ensure tight integration with Safety Services and prevent safety-critical changes from bypassing safety review.

decision.justification: The refined FR (CR-NGHE-005) is complex and non-atomic. It encompasses five distinct sub-FRs: (1) mission context ingestion, (2) performance requirement computation, (3) baseline recording, (4) history management, (5) traceability reporting. Each sub-FR requires further decomposition to identify specific DPs and IMs. The selected DP (DP-D) is sound at the system level, but the detailed design of the Performance Derivation Engine, Baseline Repository, Audit Log, and Approval Workflow requires a second decomposition level. The assigned IM (NGHE Solution) is correct, but it will need to be decomposed into sub-modules in the next level. All gates pass, indicating readiness to decompose further.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Ingest worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints) from Site Manager input via Tasking interface.
CR-NGHE-005.2: Compute performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) using mission-context-driven algorithms and validated models.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Record derived performance requirements as a baseline with metadata (timestamp, source, derivation method, approver) in a versioned repository.
CR-NGHE-005.4: Maintain a managed history of all performance requirement baselines and calibration updates with full audit trail (who, what, when, why, approval status).
CR-NGHE-005.5: Provide traceability reporting linking worksite inputs → derived requirements → baselines → history for audit and compliance.

assumptions: Worksite mission context is available in structured form (terrain map, payload mass, cycle count target, safety constraints).
Physics-based and empirical models for cycle time, energy efficiency, and availability are validated and maintained.
Site Manager and Safety Manager are available to review and approve performance requirement changes in a timely manner.
The NGHE Solution module can be decomposed into logical sub-modules (Performance Derivation Engine, Baseline Repository, Audit Log, Approval Workflow) without introducing new coupling or diffusion.
The SafetyAlert interface can be designed to support bidirectional communication between NGHE Solution and Safety Services for safety margin validation and override escalation.
open_risks: RISK: Performance derivation algorithms may not be accurate or may drift over time. MITIGATION: Validate models against real-world data; establish model maintenance and update procedures.
RISK: Approval workflow may become a bottleneck, reducing operational agility. MITIGATION: Streamline approval for routine updates; define clear decision rules and escalation paths.
RISK: Audit log may grow unbounded and consume excessive storage. MITIGATION: Implement log rotation, archival, and indexing; define retention policies.

RISK: Safety-critical changes may bypass safety review if SafetyAlert interface is not properly enforced. MITIGATION: Implement mandatory safety validation for all performance requirement changes; define escalation rules.
RISK: NGHE Solution may become a monolithic 'god module' if not properly decomposed. MITIGATION: Plan sub-module decomposition in next level; define clear interfaces between sub-modules.
evidence_needed: Detailed specification of performance derivation algorithms (cycle time model, energy model, availability model) with validation data.
Baseline repository schema and audit log format specification.
Approval workflow definition (who approves what, under what conditions, escalation rules).
SafetyAlert interface protocol specification (safety margin validation rules, constraint conflict resolution, override escalation).
Tasking interface schema specification (mission context structure and semantics).
Telemetry interface specification (performance requirement structure, baseline metadata, history summaries).
Test plan for CR-NGHE-005 verification (test cases for derivation accuracy, baseline recording, history management, traceability).
Traceability matrix linking CR-NGHE-005 sub-FRs to next-level DPs and IMs.
